channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Vance to accompany wife to Greenland amid backlash
item.description: Vance’s announcement adds another layer of tension to an already fraught atmosphere surrounding his wife’s planned visit.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Sewage sludge can find a second life on farm fields. Here's how it's made
item.description: What goes down your toilet can end up on farm fields across the United States. Biosolids, or sewage sludge, are the solid byproducts of the wastewater treatment process. Rich in nutrients, they can be used as fertilizer on agricultural fields or compost on lawns.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA A. BICKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Founders of Black-owned brands adapt their hopes and business plans for a post-DEI era
item.description: The Black creators of some consumers products are rethinking their business plans as major U.S. companies end or water down their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: Anne D'Innocenzio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: iPad submerged 5 years in London's River Thames helps unlock murder plot
item.description: The iPad was covered in dark mud after five years at the bottom of England's longest river, the Thames, but it was the chip inside that helped police crack a five-year-old murder plot.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wildfires in North and South Carolina fueled by drought, wind and fallen trees from Hurricane Helene
item.description: Dry conditions, wind and trees downed by Hurricane Helene fueled wildfires in North Carolina and South Carolina, where evacuation orders were in effect Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bryan Kohberger trial: Idaho weather from night of student murders clouds 'moon and stars' alibi
item.description: Idaho prosecutors want to use National Weather Service records in Bryan Kohberger's student murders trial that show fog, clouds and a night unfit for stargazing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LDS church applies for smaller temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching agreement; new rendering released
item.description: The LDS church says it has filed a new application for a controversial temple in Fairview, Texas, after reaching an agreement with the town.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Pilot and 2 young daughters survive the night on airplane wing after crashing into icy Alaska lake
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — A pilot and his two young daughters survived on the wing of a plane for about 12 hours after it crashed and was partially submerged in an icy Alaska lake, then were rescued after being spotted by a good Samaritan.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oscar-winning Palestinian director says Israeli soldiers beat him after attack by settlers
item.description: HEBRON, West Bank (AP) — Only a few weeks ago, Hamdan Ballal stood on a stage in Los Angeles accepting an Oscar for the film “No Other Land,” a documentary depicting his West Bank village’s struggle against Israel’s occupation.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Gunman who killed 23 in racist attack at Texas Walmart is offered plea deal to avoid death penalty
item.description: The gunman who killed 23 people in a racist attack targeting Hispanic shoppers at a Walmart near the U.S.-Mexico border in 2019 would avoid the death penalty under a plea offer announced Tuesday, abruptly ending years of efforts by prosecutors to see that he face execution by lethal injection.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A new Trump portrait for Colorado's Capitol could take time after one he disliked was removed
item.description: Filling an empty space on the wall of presidential portraits in the Colorado Capitol with a new painting of Donald Trump could take time after one he disliked was removed and put into storage.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump admin from deporting South Korean Columbia student
item.description: A federal judge Tuesday blocked the Trump administration from deporting a Columbia University student who participated in pro-Palestinian demonstrations at the school.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: Slim chance for northern lights in some states due to 'high speed stream' from Sun
item.description: Some across the U.S. may again have a chance to see the northern lights Tuesday night as material hurled out by the sun reaches Earth.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Family of Riley Strain files wrongful death lawsuit against Delta Chi fraternity
item.description: Family of Riley Strain, a University of Missouri student who died last year during a trip to Nashville, have filed a wrongful death lawsuit against his former fraternity and pledge brothers.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Ira Lee Briscoe's remains identified after Missouri man went missing in 2020
item.description: Ira Lee Briscoe had been missing for over four years, and the search for his roommate, Limon Little, continues.
item.dc:creator: Drew Tasset, NewsNation

item.title: Transgender Americans aim to block Trump's passport policy change
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When Ash Lazarus Orr went to renew his passport in early January, the transgender organizer figured it would be relatively routine. But more than two months on, Orr is waiting to get a new passport with a name change and a sex designation reflecting who he is.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: President Trump pardons former Hunter Biden business partner Devon Archer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday pardoned a former business partner of Hunter Biden who was convicted of participating in a conspiracy to defraud a Native American tribe.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs order targeting law firm that employed Mueller team prosecutor
item.description: President Trump signed an order Tuesday targeting a law firm that previously employed Andrew Weissmann, a former federal prosecutor who worked on then-special counsel Robert Mueller’s team.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: The US Postal Service has been struggling for years. Now Trump's talking about privatizing it
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service is facing an uncertain future after the resignation this week of Postmaster General Louis DeJoy and the suggestion by President Donald Trump and Elon Musk, who heads the Department of Government Efficiency, that the mail service could be privatized.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN HAIGH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen on Royal Caribbean cruise attacked by HIV-infected illegal immigrant: police
item.description: Florida police have arrested two Mexican illegal immigrants for allegedly molesting a minor on a Royal Caribbean cruise off the coast of Miami on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee pitcher Karlyn Pickens throws fastest pitch in college softball history
item.description: Tennessee softball standout Karlyn Pickens wrote her name into the history books on Monday with a record-breaking pitch.
item.dc:creator: Gregory Raucoules, WGN 9

item.title: Mexican cartel member stockpiled ammo on border
item.description: A Mexican-national member of the Cartel Del Noreste has been sentenced to five years in prison for illegally possessing ammunition on the South Texas border, U.S. Attorney Nicholas Ganjei said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Argument over Bible led to Pastor Ricky Floyd's death, attorney says
item.description: "At the other corner of that elbow was Reverend Floyd," said Perry. "She had, prior to that evening, never met Reverend Floyd.
item.dc:creator: April Thompson, NewsNation

item.title: Mega Millions is changing: What to know before its price change, game overhaul
item.description: More expensive tickets, faster-growing jackpots, and a built-in multiplier — here's what to expect when Mega Millions gets overhauled next month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: You're an American in another land? Prepare to talk about the why and how of Trump 2.0
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The urgent care doctor cocked an eyebrow at Mari Santos and her American accent. It was four days after President Donald Trump's inauguration, and Santos was a student with a stomach bug in the first weeks of an overseas semester in Glasgow, Scotland.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Diego passes first-in-the-nation policy to ban digital-only coupons at grocery stores
item.description: The San Diego City Council has passed the Grocery Pricing Transparency Ordinance, requiring grocers to offer both digital and paper coupons to all customers, promoting equal access to grocery savings opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, KTLA 5

item.title: Postmaster General Louis DeJoy resigns after 5 years in the position
item.description: The head of the beleaguered U.S. Postal Service, Louis DeJoy, resigned Monday after nearly five years in the position, leaving as President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency have floated the idea of privatizing mail service.
item.dc:creator: LISA BAUMANN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump will nominate acting CDC director Susan Monarez for the position, White House official says
item.description: President Donald Trump will nominate Susan Monarez, the acting director of the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and a longtime federal staffer, to the permanent position, a White House official confirmed Monday.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM, AMANDA SEITZ and MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump officials' war planning group chat breach
item.description: Washington was rocked Monday by a truly extraordinary story. The editor in chief of The Atlantic, Jeffrey Goldberg, recounted how he had — apparently inadvertently — been added to a group chat featuring some of the most senior members of the United States government.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: Jeffries calls for investigation of top Trump officials after bombshell military breach
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) is calling for Congress to investigate top Trump administration officials in the wake of a bombshell revelation that The Atlantic’s top editor was included in a group chat discussion detailing the Pentagon’s plans to strike Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Dairy Queen celebrates 85 years with 85-cent Blizzard deal
item.description: Dairy Queen is celebrating its 85th anniversary with a sweet deal for its loyal fans. Until April 6, DQ Rewards members can enjoy a small Blizzard Treat for just 85 cents with a minimum purchase of $1—an exclusive offer in the DQ App.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court seems divided over Louisiana case of congressional map with 2 Black majority seats
item.description: The Supreme Court seemed closely divided Monday over a challenge to Louisiana’s congressional map, which added a second Black majority district.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and SARA CLINE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Deadly hit-and-run in New Mexico brings juvenile justice challenges into focus
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Behind the wheel of the stolen car was a 12-year-old boy. In the front passenger seat was a boy who just days earlier had marked his 11th birthday.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kidnapped 6-year-old girl discovered during traffic stop: Kansas troopers
item.description: A trooper's discovery during a routine traffic stop last month led to the rescue of a 6-year-old girl who had been kidnapped for weeks, according to the Kansas Highway Patrol.
item.dc:creator: Barry Owens, Fox 5

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4
